summary: Large ftCommon header hygiene PR that removed the monolithic src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h and redistributed its declarations into narrower address-range headers such as ft_0C31.h, ft_0C88.h, ft_0CD1.h, ft_0CDD.h, ft_0CEE.h, and ft_0D14.h. Many ftCommon and character source files stopped including ft/ft_08A4.h and instead included only the specific ft headers or local ftCommon headers they needed. The PR also deleted asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s, moved its BSS symbol block into asm/melee/ft/ftdevice.s, removed ft_08A4.s.o from obj_files.mk, and gave the asm local label .L_800C4ED8 a real global symbol name ftCo_800C4ED8. No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice.

key_files.role: deleted monolithic ftCommon declaration header; changed_files reports 1115 deleted lines
deleted documentation stub/source slice; changed_files reports 970 deleted lines
new address-range header with 350 added lines of ftCo prototypes beginning at 0D14E4
new address-range header with 169 added lines of ftCo prototypes beginning at 0C318C, including ftCo_800C4ED8
expanded existing header from a small stub to 0C88xx-0CCE range declarations
new smaller header for item swing-related common action prototypes
new smaller header for attack/item shooting related common action prototypes
new smaller header for parasol-related common action prototypes
new/expanded header used where ftdevice declarations replaced ft_08A4.h
deleted asm file containing only BSS globals
received the BSS globals formerly in ft_08A4.s
renamed local asm label .L_800C4ED8 to global ftCo_800C4ED8 and updated branch targets
removed asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.o from TEXT_O_FILES after deleting the asm file
example source updated from ft_08A4.h to specific headers and corrected ftCo_800D8AE0 call to pass gobj
large include cleanup site in ftCommon initialization code

what_changed: Deleted src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h, a very large catch-all header for ftCommon declarations.

Added new address-range headers including ft_0C31.h and ft_0D14.h, and added/expanded related headers ft_0C08.h, ft_0C88.h, ft_0CD1.h, ft_0CDD.h, ft_0CEE.h, ftdevice.h, ftcolanim.h, ftattacks4combo.h, ftchangeparam.h, ftlipstickswing.h, ftmaterial.h, ftstarrodswing.h, ftmetal.h, ftafterimage.h, and ftswing.h.

Replaced many '#include "ft/ft_08A4.h"' uses across ftCommon and character files with narrower headers such as ft/ft_0C31.h, ft/ft_0C88.h, ft/ft_0D14.h, ft/ftdevice.h, and local ftCommon headers like ftCo_Wait.h, ftCo_Lift.h, and ftCo_AttackLw4.h.
Moved BSS globals ft_80459A68 through ft_8045A6A8 from deleted asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into asm/melee/ft/ftdevice.s.
Renamed asm label .L_800C4ED8 to global ftCo_800C4ED8 and updated branch/call sites in asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s and asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s.
Added explicit prototypes to several ftCommon action headers, including ftCo_CaptureDamageKoopa.h, ftCo_Guard.h, ftCo_Escape.h, ftCo_Lift.h, ftCo_ItemThrow.h, and related capture/cargo/guard headers.
Adjusted at least one prototype/callsite consistency issue: ftCo_AttackDash_IASA now calls ftCo_800D8AE0(gobj) instead of ftCo_800D8AE0().
smart_moves: Deleted the mega-header rather than leaving it as a compatibility umbrella, forcing source files to declare their true dependencies.
Split declarations by address-range headers with address-based include guards, which matches decomp navigation by DOL address and keeps future prototype additions localized.
Moved the BSS block into an existing remaining asm file before removing ft_08A4.s.o, preserving global data layout while eliminating an otherwise empty asm object.
Promoted a referenced local label to a named global symbol, ftCo_800C4ED8, making cross-file calls and prototypes possible without relying on .L labels.
Used local ftCommon headers for state-specific dependencies, e.g. ftCo_Wait.h and ftCo_Lift.h, instead of routing everything through a broad ft/ header.
Kept many unknown prototypes as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS where exact signatures were not established, avoiding overclaiming while still enabling cleaner include boundaries.
decomp_lessons: When a decomp header becomes a dumping ground, split it along natural address/file boundaries and update every source file to include only what it actually uses.
If deleting an asm object that contains only BSS/data, first relocate the symbols into a retained object and update the build list; otherwise the link/layout can silently break.
Address comments before prototypes, such as '/* 0C4ED8 */', are useful searchable anchors when names are still provisional.
A cleanup pass is a good time to expose real symbol names for asm labels that are called across file boundaries.
Forward headers like ftCommon/forward.h and dolphin mtx forward declarations can reduce dependency weight in broad action headers.
Header cleanup can reveal signature mismatches; here a call to ftCo_800D8AE0 was updated to pass gobj.
naming_conventions: ftCommon/common-fighter functions use the ftCo_ prefix, e.g. ftCo_800C4ED8, ftCo_Walk_CheckInput, ftCo_DamageSong_Anim.
Still-unknown functions keep address-derived names such as ftCo_800C318C or ft_800C80A4 rather than inventing semantic names.
Action callbacks follow the suffix convention _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, _Coll, and sometimes _Cam.
Header guards for new address-range headers use GALE01_<address>, e.g. GALE01_0C318C, GALE01_0CD140, GALE01_0D14E4.
New ft headers are named by address range, such as ft_0C31.h, ft_0CD1.h, ft_0CDD.h, ft_0CEE.h, and ft_0D14.h.
Local ftCommon state headers use ftCo_<Action>.h names, e.g. ftCo_Wait.h, ftCo_Lift.h, ftCo_Guard.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s after moving its .bss globals into asm/melee/ft/ftdevice.s.
Removed the deleted asm object from obj_files.mk to keep the build graph aligned with the new asm file layout.
Renamed .L_800C4ED8 to ftCo_800C4ED8 in asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s and updated branch links from both ft_0877.s and ft_0C31.s.
Kept raw BSS symbol names such as ft_80459A68, ft_80459A74, and ft_8045A6A8 unchanged while moving their storage, minimizing layout risk.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining includes of ft/ft_08A4.h after PR 992.
Audit ftCo prototypes still using UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS in ft_0C31.h and ft_0D14.h.
Check whether BSS globals moved to ftdevice.s later received typed C declarations.
Look for later PRs that decompiled or renamed ftCo_800C4ED8.
Search for other monolithic fighter headers that can be split by address range like ft_08A4.h.
